methodology.allergens: Essential-only tagging using the locked 16-tag aggregator vocabulary (Celery, Gluten, Crustaceans, Fish, Eggs, Lupin, Molluscs, Mustard, Nuts, Peanuts, Sesame, Soya, Sulphites, Sugared drink, Dairy, Lentils). A recipe is flagged for an allergen only when an ingredient is an unambiguous source (e.g., breaded wings/tenders/shrimp -> Gluten+Eggs+Dairy; mayo-based sauces -> Eggs; gochujang/hoisin -> Soya+Gluten+Sesame; explicit sesame seeds -> Sesame; full-sugar colas/Sprite/Fanta -> Sugared drink). No speculation. Recipes with no essential allergens are tagged ['NO_ALLERGENS'].
methodology.calories: Estimated per recipe by summing ingredient quantities x USDA per-100g kcal values (or per-piece for canned drinks/tortillas). Sub-recipe portions (wings, tenders, shrimp, ranch portion, classic fries portion) resolved to component kcal totals before summation. Combo and Fury Meal dishes inherit the union of component allergens and the sum of component kcal. Rounded to integer; wastage not applied. Estimates only — verify with lab testing for labeling.
methodology.wastage_pct: Wastage percentages stored as decimals (e.g., 0.05 = 5%). Applied at ingredient level to account for trim, cook loss, and handling waste. Sourced from Recipe Build sheet of Wing_Brands_V3_Recipes.xlsx.

changelog.entry: Enriched all recipes with allergens and calories estimated from ingredient lists.
Enriched all recipes with allergens and calories estimated from ingredient lists.

Reconciled allergens and calories identically across WoF + Winging It (shared recipe builds). Retagged using new 16-item aggregator allergen vocabulary (Celery, Gluten, Crustaceans, Fish, Eggs, Lupin, Molluscs, Mustard, Nuts, Peanuts, Sesame, Soya, Sulphites, Sugared drink, Dairy, Lentils).
Dialed back allergen tagging to strict / essential-only rule. Recipes with no essential allergens now tagged ['NO_ALLERGENS']. Arrays reconciled identically across WoF + Winging It by recipe_number.
Allergen + calorie enrichment pass applied (essential-only 16-tag vocab; USDA per-100g kcal).
